🚨 As I pointed out yesterday, another Pakistani national has been seen filming near the sensitive India Bangladesh border in the name of vlogging.

Earlier, some creators falsely projected Nepal as UP, India. Now, many Pakistani nationals are reportedly making videos near sensitive border zones and uploading them on social media.

There is nothing wrong with legal tourism. But repeated filming near strategic areas, misleading location claims, and unusual border focused content raise serious security concerns.

What looks like a casual vlog can also be used to study terrain, movement, and security patterns. India must take this seriously and raise the issue with Bangladesh and Nepal immediately.

RT by @OsintTV: मुख्यमंत्री Bhajan Lal Sharma ने नाल एयरफोर्स स्टेशन पर केंद्रीय गृहमंत्री Amit Shah की अगवानी की, बीकानेर दौरे को लेकर सुरक्षा एजेंसियां हाई अलर्ट पर; AOC नाल IAF अमित कुमार सहित वरिष्ठ सैन्य व प्रशासनिक अधिकारी रहे मौजूद, सांचू बॉर्डर पोस्ट पर BSF जवानों और ग्रामीणों से करेंगे संवाद, ‘वाइब्रेंट विलेज 2’ अभियान का करेंगे शुभारंभ

Union Home Minister Amit Shah arrived in Bikaner late Monday night on an important border-region visit and was personally received by Rajasthan Chief Minister Bhajan Lal Sharma at Nal Air Force Station. Union Law Minister Arjun Ram Meghwal accompanied the Home Minister during the visit, while senior BJP leaders, military officials, bureaucrats, and administrative officers remained present to oversee the arrangements.

Among the senior officials present were Chief Secretary V. Srinivas, DGP Rajeev Kumar Sharma, DC Vishram Meena, AOC Nal IAF Amit Kumar Budhwar, IG Om Prakash, Collector Nishant Jain, and SP Mridul Kachhawa, reflecting the high-level coordination between civil administration, security agencies, and the Indian Air Force during the visit.

Bikaner city witnessed massive security deployment, with agencies remaining on high alert from Nal Air Force Station to the BSF Headquarters. After arriving, Amit Shah directly proceeded to the BSF Headquarters, where he is scheduled for an overnight stay ahead of Tuesday’s strategic engagements.

The Union Home Minister is expected to visit the strategically significant Sanchoo border post to interact with BSF jawans and villagers living in frontier areas. He will also launch the “Vibrant Village 2” initiative focused on strengthening development, infrastructure, and connectivity in India’s border villages. Later, a high-level review meeting with senior BSF and administrative officials will take place at the BSF Headquarters in Bikaner.

The visit is being viewed as a major signal of the Centre’s continued emphasis on border security, national preparedness, and rapid development of sensitive border regions in western Rajasthan.
